name: Whole Wheat Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in the lukewarm water.
2. Scald the milk and pour it into a large bowl.
3. Add the salt, honey, and shortening to the scalded milk. Stir until the honey dissolves.
4. When the milk mixture has cooled to lukewarm, add the yeast mixture.
5. Gradually add the whole wheat flour, stirring well after each addition as the whole wheat flour absorbs water slowly.
6. When the dough is stiff enough to handle, turn it onto a floured board and knead thoroughly, gradually adding the remainder of the flour.
7. Place the dough in a greased bowl and let rise in a warm place for 2-3 hours or until doubled in bulk.
8. Punch down the dough and shape into 2 loaves.
9. Place the loaves in non-stick 9x5-inch loaf pans and allow to rise until almost doubled, about 2 hours, keeping the dough warm.
10. Bake at 325 degrees F for 1 1/2 hours, or until the loaves sound hollow when tapped with fingers.
11. Remove from pans and cool on a wire rack.
